title: relay/state: nickname_rate_limits and hpke_key_rate_limits not evicted until full disconnect
description: relay/src/state.rs: nickname_rate_limits and hpke_key_rate_limits DashMaps are only cleared on disconnect (state.disconnect when last connection for a pub_id leaves). A client that connects once, sets a nickname, then stays connected forever holds one entry — fine. But a relay running for months with many long-lived clients accumulates one entry per pub_id that ever set a nickname or published an HPKE key. Entries are never lazily evicted; they only clear on full disconnect. Contrast: rate_limits is similarly structured but resets on window expiry. For nickname/hpke rate limits (5-second cooldown), an Instant from months ago is retained indefinitely. Low impact at current scale, but for a relay with millions of connections this is unbounded growth.
